 {"id":"bd-o5xe","title":"Molecule bonding: composable workflow templates","description":"Vision: Molecules should be composable like LEGO bricks or Mad Max war rig sections. Bonding lets you attach molecules together to create compound workflows.

THREE BONDING CONTEXTS:
1. Template-time: bd mol bond A B → Create reusable compound proto
2. Spawn-time: bd mol spawn A --attach B → Attach modules when instantiating  
3. Runtime: bd mol attach epic B → Add to running workflow

BOND TYPES:
- Sequential: B after A completes (feature → deploy)
- Parallel: B runs alongside A (feature + docs)
- Conditional: B only if A fails (feature → hotfix)

BOND POINTS (Attachment Sites):
- Default: B depends on A root epic completion
- Explicit: --after issue-id for specific attachment
- Future: Named bond points in proto definitions

VARIABLE FLOW:
- Shared namespace between bonded molecules
- Warn on variable name conflicts
- Future: explicit mapping with --map

DATA MODEL: Issues track bonded_from to preserve compound lineage.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Can bond two protos into a compound proto
- Can spawn with --attach for on-the-fly composition
- Can attach molecules to running workflows
- Compound structure visible in bd mol show
- Variables flow correctly between bonded molecules","design":"SIMPLIFIED API (per design review):

CORE COMMANDS:
- bd mol spawn <proto> - Instantiate proto → molecule
- bd mol bond <A> <B> - Polymorphic bonding (any combination)
- bd mol distill <mol> - Extract molecule → proto
- bd mol show/catalog - Inspect

BOND IS POLYMORPHIC:
| bond A B      | Result                              |
|---------------|-------------------------------------|
| proto + proto | compound proto                      |
| proto + mol   | spawn proto, attach to mol          |
| mol + proto   | spawn proto, attach to mol          |
| mol + mol     | join into compound molecule         |

BOND TYPES: sequential (default), parallel, conditional

TERMINOLOGY:
- Proto: Uninstantiated template (easter egg: 'protomolecule')
- Molecule: Spawned instance of a proto
- Compound: Result of bonding (proto or molecule)
- Distill: Extract proto from ad-hoc epic","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:58:35.479009-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T01:10:24.298798-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-o91r","title":"Polymorphic bond command: bd mol bond A B","description":"Implement proto-to-proto bonding to create compound protos.

COMMAND: bd mol bond proto-feature proto-testing [--as proto-feature-tested] [--type sequential]

BEHAVIOR:
- Load both proto subgraphs
- Create new compound proto with combined structure
- B's root becomes child of A's root (sequential) or sibling (parallel)
- Wire dependencies: B depends on A's leaf nodes (sequential) or runs parallel
- Store bonded_from metadata for lineage tracking

FLAGS:
- --as NAME: Custom ID for compound proto (default: generates hash)
- --type: sequential (default) or parallel
- --dry-run: Preview compound structure

OUTPUT:
- New compound proto in catalog
- Shows combined variable requirements","notes":"UPDATE: bond is now polymorphic - handles proto+proto, proto+mol, and mol+mol based on operand types. Separate 'attach' command eliminated.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:58:55.604705-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T10:10:25.385995-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T10:10:25.385995-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented in commit f0df4070","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-o91r","depends_on_id":"bd-o5xe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.30026-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-o91r","depends_on_id":"bd-mh4w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.569391-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-o91r","depends_on_id":"bd-rnnr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.652397-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-o9o","title":"Exclude pinned issues from bd ready","description":"Update bd ready to exclude pinned issues. Pinned issues are context markers, not work items, and should never appear in the ready-to-work list.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T23:33:41.979073-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T23:33:41.979073-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-o9o","depends_on_id":"bd-0vg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T23:33:56.392931-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-o9o","depends_on_id":"bd-7h5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T23:34:07.612655-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-obep","title":"Spawn-time bonding: --attach flag","description":"Add --attach flag to bd mol spawn for on-the-fly composition.

COMMAND: bd mol spawn proto-feature --attach proto-docs --attach proto-testing

BEHAVIOR:
- Spawn the primary proto as normal
- For each --attach: spawn that proto and wire to primary
- Attachments become children of primary's root epic
- Dependencies wired based on bond type (default: sequential)

FLAGS:
- --attach PROTO: Attach a proto (can repeat)
- --attach-type TYPE: sequential (default) or parallel for all attachments
- --after ISSUE: Attachment point for attached protos

VARIABLE HANDLING:
- All attached protos share variable namespace
- Warn on variable name conflicts
- All --var flags apply to all protos","notes":"DESIGN NOTE: This is syntactic sugar. Equivalent to:
  bd mol spawn proto-A
  bd mol bond $new_epic_id proto-B
  
Keeping as separate task because it's a common UX pattern worth optimizing.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:06.178092-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T01:10:42.934108-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-obep","depends_on_id":"bd-o5xe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.368491-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-obep","depends_on_id":"bd-o91r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.733369-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

VARIABLE HANDLING:

 {"id":"bd-of2p","title":"Improve version bump molecule with missing steps","description":"During v0.32.1 release, discovered missing steps in the release molecule:

**Missing from molecule:**
1. Rebuild ~/go/bin/bd (only did ~/.local/bin/bd)
2. Install beads-mcp from local source: `uv tool install --reinstall ./integrations/beads-mcp`
3. Restart daemons with `bd daemons killall`
4. (Optional) Publish beads-mcp to PyPI

**Current molecule steps (bd-6s61):**
1. Update CHANGELOG.md
2. Update info.go versionChanges  
3. Run bump-version.sh
4. Run tests and linting
5. Update local installation
6. Commit and push release
7. Wait for CI
8. Verify release artifacts

**Proposed additions:**
- After "Update local installation": rebuild BOTH ~/.local/bin/bd AND ~/go/bin/bd
- Add: "Install beads-mcp from source" step
- Add: "Restart daemons" step
- Add: "Verify all versions match" step that checks all artifacts

**Also learned:**
- Must run from mayor/rig to avoid git conflicts with bd sync (already documented in bump-version.sh)","notes":"CORRECTION: npm publishing IS automated and working!

**Package naming:**
- OLD: `beads` (npm) - deprecated, stuck at 0.2.1
- CURRENT: `@beads/bd` (npm) - scoped package, auto-published by CI

**How it works:**
- CI uses OIDC trusted publishing (no token needed)
- Workflow: .github/workflows/release.yml → publish-npm job
- Permissions: `id-token: write` enables GitHub OIDC
- To install: `npm install -g @beads/bd` (not `npm install beads`)

**All publishing is automated on tag push:**
1. GitHub Release - goreleaser ✓
2. PyPI - publish-pypi job ✓
3. Homebrew - update-homebrew job ✓
4. npm (@beads/bd) - publish-npm job ✓

**Remaining molecule improvements (local steps only):**
- Rebuild BOTH ~/.local/bin/bd AND ~/go/bin/bd
- Install beads-mcp from source: `uv tool install --reinstall ./integrations/beads-mcp`
- Restart daemons: `bd daemons killall`
- Run from mayor/rig to avoid git conflicts with bd sync
- Final verification step to check all local versions match","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/dave","created_at":"2025-12-20T22:09:11.845787-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T22:35:53.687587-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-ola6","title":"Implement transaction retry logic for SQLITE_BUSY","description":"BEGIN IMMEDIATE fails immediately on SQLITE_BUSY instead of retrying with exponential backoff.

Location: internal/storage/sqlite/sqlite.go:223-225

Problem:
- Under concurrent write load, BEGIN IMMEDIATE can fail with SQLITE_BUSY
- Current implementation fails immediately instead of retrying
- Results in spurious failures under normal concurrent usage

Solution: Implement exponential backoff retry:
- Retry up to N times (e.g., 5)
- Backoff: 10ms, 20ms, 40ms, 80ms, 160ms
- Check for context cancellation between retries
- Only retry on SQLITE_BUSY/database locked errors

Impact: Spurious failures under concurrent write load

Effort: 3 hours","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-16T14:51:31.247147-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-16T14:51:31.247147-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-ork0","title":"Add comments to 30+ silently ignored errors or fix them","description":"Code health review found 30+ instances of error suppression using blank identifier without explanation:

Good examples (with comments):
- merge.go: _ = gitRmCmd.Run() // Ignore errors
- daemon_watcher.go: _ = watcher.Add(...) // Ignore error

Bad examples (no context):
- create.go:213: dbPrefix, _ = store.GetConfig(ctx, "issue_prefix")
- daemon_sync_branch.go: _ = daemonClient.Close()
- migrate_hash_ids.go, version_tracking.go: _ = store.Close()

Fix: Add comments explaining WHY errors are ignored, or handle them properly.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:17:25.899372-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T18:17:25.899372-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ork0","depends_on_id":"bd-tggf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:19:06.275843-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
